blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: I think Davenport labeled it suspicious for a reason. And I think we're sitting here trying to talk ourselves into natural causes because the alternative is something none of us wants to say out loud.

blocks.speaker: Nadia
blocks.content: Someone in his life wanted him dead. And this weekend gave them the opportunity.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: His life. Not necessarily someone in this house.

blocks.speaker: Cade
blocks.content: The phone call. Whoever he was arguing with Saturday night.

blocks.speaker: Dean
blocks.content: Exactly. It could be someone completely outside this group. A business deal gone wrong. Someone we've never met.

She kept coming back to that. It was the thing she couldn't get past and honestly nobody else could either. If the threat was external — if it was a business deal, a stranger, someone in Marcus's world they didn't know — then five grieving friends were irrelevant. You let them go home. You didn't hold them at a beach house while you figured it out.

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: Because we're the ones who were here.
She said it simply. Like the answer had been obvious all along.

blocks.speaker: Wren
blocks.content: We're the only people who were with him. Of course they're not letting us leave.
